assertions.text: Output contains at least one markdown table
assertions.evidence:
| Scenario | Current Status | Target Status | Allowed? | Reason? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Normal confirmation | PENDING | CONFIRMED | yes |

assertions.text: Table covers cancellation rules — includes rows for states where cancellation is allowed and where it is not
assertions.evidence: Cancel before shipment | {PENDING, CONFIRMED} | CANCELLED | yes | and Cancel after dispatch | SHIPPED | CANCELLED | no | Already dispatched and Cancel delivered order | DELIVERED | CANCELLED | no | Use return process instead

assertions.text: The 30-day return window rule is addressed — either as a threshold column, a separate table, or flagged as an open question
assertions.evidence: Table 2: Return Eligibility | Scenario | Order Status | Days Since Delivery | Return Window (Policy) | Eligible? | and Last day of window | DELIVERED | 30 | 30 | ? | **Open: is day 30 inclusive?**

assertions.text: Uses value sets {PENDING, CONFIRMED} or equivalent to express 'regardless of which starting state' for cases where a rule holds across states
assertions.evidence: Cancel before shipment | {PENDING, CONFIRMED} | CANCELLED | yes and Not yet delivered | {PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED} | | 30 | no

assertions.text: At least one output column ends with '?'
assertions.evidence: Column header 'Allowed?' and 'Eligible?' both end with '?'. Values include '?' such as: Reopen cancelled order | CANCELLED | PENDING | ? | **Open: is re-opening allowed?**
